title: Smoky Velvety Norwegian Salmon Curry
description: A midnight‑craving Norwegian dish that pairs smoky tandoor‑roasted salmon with a velvety curry sauce, enriched with cream and aromatic spices.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (392°F). Season salmon fillets with salt, pepper, ½ tsp smoked paprika, and a drizzle of olive oil. Roast on a baking sheet for 12–15 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and ginger; cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Add ground cumin, turmeric, fenugreek, garam masala, and remaining smoked paprika. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and heavy cream, bring to a gentle simmer. Let thicken slightly, about 5 minutes, adding a splash of water if too thick.
Slice roasted salmon into bite‑size pieces and gently fold into the curry. Simmer for 3–4 minutes to heat through.
Finish with lime juice, taste, and adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed. Stir in chopped cilantro.
Serve hot over steamed rice or cauliflower rice, garnished with extra cilantro.
